title: Should Boston host the 2028 Democratic National Convention?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston is a finalist among five cities for hosting the 2028 Democratic National Convention.
Officials state the convention could generate up to $400 million in economic benefits for Boston.
Local leaders highlight Boston's historic significance and infrastructure as advantages for hosting the event.

title: Early thoughts on the Celtics’ offseason: Red flags, silver linings, and biggest needs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics blew a 3-1 series lead in playoffs, facing offseason challenges with coach Joe Mazzulla's surprising 56-win season.
Team's heavy reliance on 3-point shooting backfired; lost key shooters Jrue Holiday, Al Horford, and Kristaps Porzingis.
Celtics seek scoring diversity including a scoring big man and veteran two-guard to strengthen offensive options.

title: 21-year-old woman charged after wrong-way crash with N.H. State Police cruiser
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cassandra Aldecoa, 21, was charged following a wrong-way crash with a state trooper on Route 101 in Exeter, N.H.
Aldecoa faces felony charges including reckless conduct and second-degree assault, along with DUI-related misdemeanors.
Passenger Zachary Lapierre, 21, faces charges such as disorderly conduct; he was released, with a July 7 court date.

title: Franklin Park Zoo is getting two new gorillas as Little Joe heads to Pittsburgh
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Franklin Park Zoo's 33-year-old gorilla, Little Joe, will move to Pittsburgh Zoo as part of a breeding program.
Two young gorillas, Frankie from Pittsburgh and Moke from Washington, D.C., will join Franklin Park Zoo later this year.
Little Joe's departure allows the formation of a bachelor group with existing 5-year-old gorilla, Pablo, at Franklin Park Zoo.

title: Person medflighted after falling from zoo ride in Mendon
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man experienced a seizure and fell from the Skyfari Sky Ride at Southwick Zoo in Mendon, Massachusetts.
The man was airlifted by UMass Lifeflight to a hospital in Worcester following the incident.
The Skyfari Sky Ride is temporarily closed for inspection and safety review, although no malfunction was reported.

title: New details emerge as Mass. McDonald’s employee accused of giving her ex-girlfriend contaminated fries
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Kaylie Santos allegedly spat into and ate fries before serving them to her ex-girlfriend at a Southbridge McDonald's.
Santos, charged with distributing contaminated food, was caught on surveillance and a private Snapchat video from April 9.
A no-trespass order was filed against Santos by the franchise operators; she is scheduled for arraignment on June 5.

title: Odds are increasing for a ‘super El Niño’ this summer. Here’s what that means for Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A potential 'super' El Niño could develop this year, possibly making it one of the strongest on record.
Massachusetts might experience a hotter, more humid summer with reduced hurricane activity due to the El Niño event.
El Niño impacts include altered global weather patterns, affecting regions like the Amazon and southern U.S. with droughts or heavy rain.

title: Billy Eichner talks Conan, Amy Poehler, Madonna – and why he is the way he is
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Billy Eichner releases his audiobook memoir 'Billy on Billy' on May 19, detailing his upbringing and career.
The memoir features Eichner's natural voice and focuses on his happy childhood and supportive parents in Queens.
Eichner's memoir tour stops at Boston's Wilbur Theatre on May 21 with special guests and includes a free audiobook.

title: Michette named among the best French bakeries in the U.S., according to Tasting Table
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Michette in Somerville was named one of the 14 best French bakeries in the U.S. by Tasting Table.
The bakery is praised for its flaky croissants, cookies, and creative use of classic pastry techniques.
Noteworthy items include twice-baked croissants and chocolate chunk cookies with buckwheat and fleur de sel.

title: Steven Tyler stops for ice cream in Quincy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Aerosmith's Steven Tyler visited Bella's Creamery in Quincy for ice cream.
Tyler, 78, posed for a selfie with a store employee holding an ice cream cone.
Bella's Creamery's Facebook post of Tyler's visit gained over 3,800 likes.
